draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer persistent navigation over tabbed navigation for AI workspace context switching
facade.label: Sidebar with floating panels
facade.acceptImplies: Explore sidebar-anchored layouts with floating/detachable panels for tools and context
facade.rejectImplies: Explore tab-based or top navigation with full-screen modal switching

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer persistent navigation over tabbed navigation for AI workspace context switching
evidence.implication: Explore sidebar-anchored layouts with floating/detachable panels for tools and context

facade.hypothesis: Users of an AI workspace prefer a friendly, approachable voice that feels like talking to a knowledgeable colleague rather than reading documentation
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into warmth, first-person language, casual phrasing, and personality in UI copy and error messages
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward formal, neutral, instructional language that prioritizes clarity and authority over rapport

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer quantified metrics over narrative summaries in their workspace interface
facade.label: Dashboard numbers
facade.acceptImplies: Favor KPI cards, stat blocks, and numeric displays for quick information scanning
facade.rejectImplies: Favor descriptive text, explanations, and contextual storytelling over raw numbers

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer low-light interfaces to reduce cognitive load during long coding/thinking sessions
facade.label: Dark mode
facade.acceptImplies: Explore darker palettes, high-contrast text, reduced eye strain as a design value
facade.rejectImplies: Favor bright, airy, well-lit interfaces; darkness may feel isolating or heavy

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer interactive elements that smoothly transform into new states rather than swapping instantly
facade.axisTargeted: Transition continuity—do UI elements flow between states or snap?
facade.label: Buttons that morph
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should prioritize fluid, shape-shifting interactions over instant state changes; morphing confirms motion as a core value
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace favors crisp, instantaneous feedback; morphing feels gratuitous and users want immediate visual clarity over motion continuity

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants bright energetic workspace vs calm focused environment for concentration
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple summaries and key metrics only
Whether user wants instant responsive feedback vs smooth gradual transitions and animations

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants bright energetic workspace vs calm focused environment for concentration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple summaries and key metrics only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant responsive feedback vs smooth gradual transitions and animations
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces may prefer diffused, layered lighting over harsh contrast—suggesting a preference for calm, approachable interfaces
facade.label: Soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace favors gentle, human-scaled aesthetics; probably wants organic shapes, muted colors, and breathing room
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace leans toward clean minimalism, high contrast, or clinical precision; probably prefers bold edges and stark visual hierarchy

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer visual card layouts over linear lists for organizing multiple AI tools and contexts simultaneously
facade.axisTargeted: Information architecture density and scanability — grid vs list presentation
facade.label: Cards in a grid
facade.acceptImplies: Explore modular card systems, visual hierarchy through spacing and imagery, dashboard-style layouts for AI workspace tools
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer sequential/linear organization; test list-based or stream-style layouts instead; reconsider grid's cognitive load

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
